title: aesculus hippocastanum f. luteovariegatum
intro: Aesculus hippocastanum f. luteovariegatum, also known as the Horse Chestnut, is a variegated cultivar of the Aesculus hippocastanum tree, native to the Balkans and southern Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain aescin, a saponin compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Horse Chestnut include reducing inflammation and swelling in the veins, improving blood flow, and alleviating symptoms of varicose veins, spider veins, and hemorrhoids. In herbal preparations, Horse Chestnut seeds are often used in tinctures, capsules, and topical creams, and can also be consumed as a tea to support overall vascular health.
